title: Cryo-tomography sub-lamellar density modulation
statement: A sub-lamellar chain-end density modulation (≈3 nm) underlies the residual spacing offset and is resolvable only by cryogenic electron tomography.
what_it_explains: the hypothesized molecular origin of the offset between measured and thermodynamic spacing
knowledge_gap: no direct real-space evidence for sub-lamellar modulation exists
predicted_outcome: a 3 nm periodic density feature inside each lamella
novelty_basis: never imaged directly in these systems

title: Steady shear raises equilibrium domain spacing
statement: Under steady shear above a threshold rate, the post-anneal equilibrium domain spacing increases monotonically with shear rate via convective chain stretching — i.e. L0 = f(T, shear), not f(T) alone.
what_it_explains: the shear-dependence term the consensus thermodynamic model omits — directly tests the detected anomaly
knowledge_gap: consensus treats shear as orientation-only; equilibrium shear dependence is unquantified
predicted_outcome: domain spacing rises ~1.9·log(1+shear) nm, reproducible after full annealing
novelty_basis: contradicts the reviewed consensus with an equilibrium (not transient) claim

title: Thermal-history hysteresis in spacing
statement: Domain spacing exhibits measurable hysteresis between heating and cooling branches, indicating a kinetically trapped contribution.
what_it_explains: whether the offset is kinetic (trapped) rather than thermodynamic
predicted_outcome: a >0.3 nm gap between heating and cooling branches
novelty_basis: kinetic framing of an assumed-equilibrium quantity

title: Near-surface vs bulk spacing divergence
statement: The surface field pins a distinct near-surface spacing that diverges from the bulk value by more than instrument resolution.
what_it_explains: spatial heterogeneity of spacing within a film
knowledge_gap: depth dependence of L0 under-characterised in thin films

title: Substrate-induced depth gradient
statement: The buried substrate interface imposes a monotonic depth-dependent spacing gradient detectable by neutron reflectometry.
what_it_explains: substrate contribution to the spacing offset
knowledge_gap: buried-interface depth profiles scarce for these films
predicted_outcome: a graded spacing rising toward the substrate
novelty_basis: depth gradient not previously resolved here

title: SCFT with nonequilibrium shear term
statement: Self-consistent field theory augmented with a convective (nonequilibrium) shear term reproduces the measured L0(T, shear) surface within error.
what_it_explains: a unifying mechanistic model spanning temperature and shear
knowledge_gap: no published SCFT couples shear convection to equilibrium spacing
predicted_outcome: simulated surface matches data to <0.25 nm RMS
novelty_basis: borrows a convective term from rheology into SCFT

title: Dispersity broadens spacing distribution
statement: Increased molecular-weight dispersity broadens the SAXS primary-peak width, indicating a wider distribution of local domain spacings.
what_it_explains: the link between polydispersity and spacing heterogeneity
predicted_outcome: peak FWHM grows with dispersity index
nearest_paper: Chen et al. 2024, Macromolecules — 'Dispersity control of lamellar period distributions' (already reports this effect)
